title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 30 August 2024.

items.title: **Konami** acknowledges that **Metal Gear Solid 4** is still only playable on PS3
bullets: When asked about it, producer Noriaki Okamura said "with [Metal Gear Solid] Vol 1 containing MGS 1-3 dot dot dot… you can probably connect the dots"

items.title: **Annapurna** is co-financing **Control 2**, will bring Remedy IP including Alan Wake to film and TV
bullets: Annapurna will give 50% of the budget for Control 2
Remedy will receive a greater share of game sales revenue after initial investments are recouped, while Annapurna will receive a greater share from audiovisual productions

items.title: **Harry Potter: Magic Awakened** to shut down
bullets: Officially close its operations in the Americas, Europe and Oceania territories on October 29, 2024 and will be removed from app stores on August 26, 2024

items.title: **Games Done Quick** officially under new ownership
bullets: Matt Merkle now owns majority of GDQ, leadership team includes Jason Deng and Ashley Farkas

items.title: **Final Fantasy: Brave Exvius** will end global service on 30th October
items.body: Once closed, all player accounts will be deleted.
bullets: The game was released worldwide in 2016, following its Japanese launch in 2015. The Japanese version will remain in service.
In-game events are scheduled to be continually updated until the app service end on 30th October.
